questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a 'Full Custom' IC design methodology?
options: Every transistor and interconnect is manually designed and optimized.
It relies exclusively on pre-designed standard cells for all logic.
It primarily uses pre-verified IP blocks without modification to accelerate design.
It involves designing only the high-level architecture using Hardware Description Languages.
It exclusively uses Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) for rapid prototyping.

questions.question: What is the primary difference in how instruction-level parallelism (ILP) is exposed and managed between a Superscalar processor and a Very Long Instruction Word (VLIW) processor?
options: Superscalar processors dynamically detect parallelism at runtime, while VLIW processors rely on the compiler to statically bundle independent operations.
VLIW processors are designed for a single instruction stream, whereas Superscalar processors support multiple instruction streams concurrently.
Superscalar processors require explicit programmer annotations for parallel execution, unlike VLIW processors.
VLIW processors execute only one instruction per clock cycle, whereas Superscalar processors execute many.
Superscalar processors feature simpler instruction decoders compared to VLIW processors due to fixed instruction formats.

questions.question: In digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of inserting 'Scan Chains' into a design during the Design For Testability (DFT) phase?
options: To allow for structural testing of internal sequential logic elements by shifting test patterns in and out.
To reduce the overall area overhead of the silicon die by optimizing placement.
To improve the functional performance and operating frequency of the circuit.
To enable dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) for power management.
To synchronize data transfers between asynchronous clock domains.

questions.question: In a multi-processor system with shared memory, what is the fundamental distinction between 'cache coherence' and 'memory consistency'?
options: Cache coherence ensures that all processors see a consistent view of a *single memory block*, while memory consistency defines the *order* in which memory operations from different processors are observed by all processors.
Memory consistency applies only to on-chip caches (L1/L2), whereas cache coherence applies to the main memory (DRAM).
Cache coherence protocols are implemented in software, while memory consistency models are purely hardware-driven.
Memory consistency is concerned with data locality, while cache coherence focuses on overall system throughput.
There is no functional distinction; they are two terms for the exact same concept of ensuring correct data across processors.

questions.question: In the final physical verification stages of an Integrated Circuit, what is the primary role of an 'Electrical Rule Check' (ERC)?
options: To identify electrical issues in the design, such as floating nets, power/ground shorts, or improper connection of power domains.
To verify that the physical layout adheres to manufacturing process geometric constraints and tolerances.
To compare the extracted physical layout against the original logical schematic to ensure functional equivalence.
To predict the timing performance of critical paths and verify setup/hold times.
To perform functional simulation of the entire chip under various test scenarios.
